articles.title: 2026 Paint Color Trends: Warm Earth Tones & Soft Blacks
articles.excerpt: See which paint colors designers are calling must-haves for 2026, from cozy browns to sophisticated soft blacks.
articles.content: Designers are predicting a continued shift toward warm, grounded palettes in 2026. Expect to see clay-inspired browns, rich camel, and soft black accents replacing cooler grays...

articles.title: 2025 Interior Paint Color Trends for Modern Homes
articles.excerpt: From moody greens to muted terracotta, explore the top interior paint color trends shaping 2025.
articles.content: The 2025 interior palette focuses on livable luxury: layered neutrals, moody greens, and muted terracotta accents. In living rooms, designers are favoring rich olive and eucalyptus tones...

articles.title: 2024 Kitchen Paint Color Trends: Greens, Blues, and Beyond
articles.excerpt: Thinking about updating your kitchen? These 2024 paint color trends balance style and practicality.
articles.content: Kitchens in 2024 are leaning into character-rich hues. Sage greens and inky blues dominate lower cabinets, while warm white and putty tones keep upper cabinets light...

articles.title: Small Apartment Color Ideas That Don’t Feel Cramped
articles.excerpt: Learn how to use color in a 1-bedroom apartment to make spaces feel brighter and larger.
articles.content: Color can dramatically change how large or small your apartment feels. In compact living rooms, painting walls and ceilings in a soft, continuous neutral elongates sightlines...

articles.title: Exterior Paint Maintenance Guide for Coastal Homes
articles.excerpt: Salt air can be tough on paint. Learn maintenance strategies to extend the life of your exterior finish.
articles.content: Coastal environments expose paint films to salt, UV, and wind-driven rain. Annual inspections are essential. Start by checking south- and west-facing elevations for chalking...

articles.title: Eco-Friendly & Low-VOC Paints: What Homeowners Should Know
articles.excerpt: Understand the differences between zero-VOC, low-VOC, and conventional paints before your next project.
articles.content: Low- and zero-VOC paints significantly reduce indoor air pollutants during and after painting. However, VOC ratings only measure certain solvents, not all possible emissions...

articles.title: Choosing Brand-Right Paint Colors for Retail & Offices
articles.excerpt: Align your interior and exterior paint colors with your brand without overwhelming customers or staff.
articles.content: Brand colors are powerful, but using them wall-to-wall can be visually exhausting. A better strategy is to anchor key accent walls and customer touchpoints in your core shades...

articles.title: How to Choose the Right Paint Finish for Every Room
articles.excerpt: Matte, eggshell, satin, or semi-gloss? Here’s how to pick the best finish for each surface.
articles.content: Paint finish affects durability, cleanability, and how wall imperfections appear. In high-traffic hallways, eggshell is typically the sweet spot between wipeability and glare...

articles.title: Deck Staining 101: Timing, Prep, and Color Selection
articles.excerpt: A step-by-step guide to cleaning, prepping, and staining your deck for durable results.
articles.content: Proper deck staining starts with moisture testing and cleaning. Surfaces must be dry to below 15% moisture content before applying stain...

articles.title: Office Paint Colors That Support Focus and Productivity
articles.excerpt: Explore research-backed color palettes that help teams stay energized without feeling overstimulated.
articles.content: Studies suggest that muted blues and blue-greens can support sustained focus, while warm neutrals in collaboration zones encourage conversation...

articles.title: Boosting Retail Curb Appeal with Exterior Paint
articles.excerpt: Learn how strategic exterior colors and finishes can draw more customers into your store.
articles.content: First impressions begin at the sidewalk. High-chroma doors, clean trim lines, and well-maintained fascias communicate that a business is active and cared for...

articles.title: Can You Paint a House Exterior in Winter?
articles.excerpt: Cold-weather formulas and timing tips for extending exterior painting season.
articles.content: Modern exterior paints can often be applied down to 35°F, but surface and air temperatures must be monitored carefully...

articles.title: 2022 Paint Color Trends Recap: What Stayed and What Faded
articles.excerpt: A look back at the paint color trends that defined 2022 and how they aged.
articles.content: 2022 was the year of soft greens and grounded mid-tone blues. While some hues remain timeless, others already feel dated...

articles.title: Interior vs. Exterior Paint: What’s the Difference?
articles.excerpt: Not all paints are interchangeable. Learn why interior and exterior formulas are built differently.
articles.content: Exterior paints are engineered to resist UV, temperature swings, and moisture, while interior paints prioritize stain resistance and washability...

articles.title: Renter-Friendly Paint Color Trends You Can Actually Use
articles.excerpt: Soft neutrals and subtle color shifts landlords love—and you won’t get charged for later.
articles.content: For rentals, the safest approach is to stay within one or two steps of the existing neutral. Greige, soft white, and pale blue-greens feel fresh without alarming property managers...
